mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate applied to corporate earnings, increasing present values of future cash flows and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 4.0% signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0%, or the signal loses predictive power during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal spending and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand and construction sector growth, compounding corporate revenue expectations and yielding positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline model, with 60-day forward returns showing negative alpha in over 60% of co-expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which directly boosts corporate profit margins and generates positive monthly equity returns during supply-led growth phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 20-day returns during supply-led recovery periods, with a t-statistic below 1.5 on the coefficient.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls demonstrate sustained job creation and rising household income, supporting consumer discretionary spending and driving positive short-term S&P 500 momentum.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 5-day forward returns across multiple market cycles, invalidating the employment-growth premium.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index levels surpassing 260.0 indicate entrenched inflationary pressures that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative quarterly market returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in over 65% of periods where CPIAUCSL remains above 260.0, or the inflation threshold loses predictive accuracy.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and real personal spending confirms synchronized macroeconomic expansion, reducing recession probabilities and generating positive monthly equity returns through improved earnings forecasts.
failure_condition: The combined GDPC1 and RSAFS growth signal yields negative or flat 20-day returns in more than half of synchronized expansion quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining industrial production and stagnant payroll growth signals manufacturing sector contraction and impending layoffs, triggering rapid risk aversion and negative weekly S&P 500 returns during supply shocks.
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict negative 5-day returns during manufacturing downturns, with positive returns occurring in over 50% of cases.
